title_en: Taharah in Al-Kafi: Hadith-Grounded Maliki Purity Law
title_id: Taharah dalam Al-Kafi: Hukum Kesucian Maliki Berlandaskan Hadis
content_en:
The taharah chapter of al-Kafi presents Maliki purity law with Ibn Abd al-Barr's characteristic addition of hadith grounding. Where other Maliki texts might state the school's positions without extended reference to evidence, Ibn Abd al-Barr's mastery of hadith allows him to connect each major ruling to its prophetic basis.

On water, al-Kafi presents the Maliki positions with reference to the hadith evidence. The ruling that seawater is pure and purifying is grounded in the well-known hadith: 'Its water is pure and its dead are lawful,' a response the Prophet gave to a man who asked about performing wudu with seawater while on a sea voyage. Ibn Abd al-Barr notes that this hadith is sound and that Imam Malik had specific access to the practice of Medina's traders who regularly used seawater for purification when traveling by sea.

The wudu chapter presents the Maliki obligations with their evidential basis. On the requirement of wiping the entire head, Ibn Abd al-Barr notes the Quranic verse (5:6) which uses the Arabic formulation 'wipe your heads,' and Malik's interpretation that the definite article (al-) indicates the whole head is meant. He presents the evidential disagreement between the Maliki interpretation and the minority reading that permits wiping only part of the head, and explains Malik's reasoning for the majority Maliki position.

On the Maliki requirement of muwalah (continuity) in wudu — performing the obligatory elements one after another without significant interruption — Ibn Abd al-Barr presents the evidence from the practice of the Prophet and the Companions, which Malik regarded as establishing continuity as an obligation rather than merely a sunnah. The practical question of how long an interruption is 'significant' is addressed through the principle of custom: an interruption that causes the earlier washed parts to dry before the later ones are washed is too long.

For ghusl, Ibn Abd al-Barr presents the hadith on the obligating causes and the minimum valid performance. His characteristic approach is to note the sound hadiths that establish each ruling and to explain how Malik interpreted them. On the question of whether the ghusl of janabah and the Friday ghusl may be combined in one bath, he presents Malik's position (they may be combined with a single intention for both) with the evidential basis.

The chapter on hayd (menstruation) is presented with the clarity that reflects Ibn Abd al-Barr's awareness of its practical importance. The Maliki positions on the minimum and maximum durations of hayd, the characteristics that distinguish menstrual blood from other types of bleeding, and the acts forbidden to a menstruating woman are all stated with their evidential grounding.
content_id:
Bab taharah dalam al-Kafi menyajikan hukum kesucian mazhab Maliki dengan ciri khas Ibn Abd al-Barr berupa landasan hadis. Apabila teks-teks Maliki lainnya mungkin hanya menyatakan posisi mazhab tanpa mengacu secara luas kepada dalil, penguasaan Ibn Abd al-Barr atas hadis memungkinkannya menghubungkan setiap ketentuan hukum utama dengan dasar kenabiannya.

Mengenai air, al-Kafi menyajikan posisi mazhab Maliki dengan mengacu pada dalil hadis. Hukum bahwa air laut adalah suci dan menyucikan didasarkan pada hadis terkenal: Airnya suci dan bangkainya halal, yaitu jawaban yang diberikan Nabi kepada seseorang yang bertanya tentang berwudu dengan air laut ketika berada di atas kapal. Ibn Abd al-Barr mencatat bahwa hadis ini sahih dan bahwa Imam Malik memiliki pengetahuan langsung tentang praktik para pedagang Madinah yang secara rutin menggunakan air laut untuk bersuci saat bepergian melalui laut.

Bab wudu menyajikan kewajiban-kewajiban dalam mazhab Maliki beserta dasar dalilnya. Mengenai kewajiban mengusap seluruh kepala, Ibn Abd al-Barr menyebutkan ayat Al-Quran (5:6) yang menggunakan ungkapan Arab usaplah kepala kalian, serta penafsiran Imam Malik bahwa kata sandang tertentu (al-) mengindikasikan maksudnya adalah seluruh kepala. Ia memaparkan perbedaan dalil antara penafsiran Maliki dan pendapat minoritas yang membolehkan mengusap hanya sebagian kepala, serta menjelaskan alasan Imam Malik untuk posisi mayoritas Maliki tersebut.

Mengenai persyaratan muwalah dalam mazhab Maliki, yakni melakukan unsur-unsur wajib wudu secara berurutan tanpa jeda yang berarti, Ibn Abd al-Barr menyajikan dalil dari praktik Nabi dan para Sahabat, yang dianggap oleh Imam Malik sebagai penetapan kewajiban kesinambungan tersebut dan bukan sekadar sunnah. Pertanyaan praktis tentang berapa lama jeda dianggap berarti dijawab melalui prinsip kebiasaan: jeda yang menyebabkan anggota yang lebih awal dicuci mengering sebelum anggota berikutnya dicuci dianggap terlalu lama.

Untuk ghusl, Ibn Abd al-Barr menyajikan hadis-hadis mengenai sebab-sebab yang mewajibkannya dan pelaksanaan minimum yang sah. Pendekatan khasnya adalah mencatat hadis-hadis sahih yang menetapkan setiap ketentuan dan menjelaskan cara Imam Malik menafsirkannya. Mengenai apakah ghusl janabah dan ghusl Jumat dapat digabungkan dalam satu kali mandi, ia menyajikan posisi Imam Malik bahwa keduanya dapat digabungkan dengan satu niat untuk keduanya, beserta dasar dalilnya.

Bab mengenai haid disajikan dengan kejelasan yang mencerminkan kesadaran Ibn Abd al-Barr akan kepentingan praktisnya. Posisi mazhab Maliki tentang durasi minimum dan maksimum haid, karakteristik yang membedakan darah haid dari jenis pendarahan lainnya, serta perbuatan-perbuatan yang dilarang bagi wanita yang sedang haid, semuanya dinyatakan beserta landasannya secara dalil.
content_ar: The الطهارة الباب of al-Kafi presents المالكي purity law with Ibn Abd al-Barr's characteristic addition of الحديث grounding. Where other المالكي texts might state the school's positions without extended reference to evidence, Ibn Abd al-Barr's mastery of الحديث allows him to connect each major ruling to its prophetic basis. On water, al-Kafi presents the المالكي positions with reference to the الحديث evidence. The ruling that seawater is pure and purifying is grounded in the well-known الحديث: 'Its water is pure and its dead are lawful,' a response النبي ﷺ gave to a man who asked about performing الوضوء with seawater بينما on a sea voyage. Ibn Abd al-Barr notes that this الحديث is sound and that الإمام مالك had specific access to the practice of المدينة المنورة's traders who regularly used seawater for purification عندما traveling by sea. The الوضوء الباب presents the المالكي obligations with their evidential basis. On the requirement of wiping the entire head, Ibn Abd al-Barr notes the القرآني الآية (5:6) which uses the Arabic formulation 'wipe your heads,' and مالك's interpretation that the definite article (al-) indicates the whole head is meant. He presents the evidential disagreement between the المالكي interpretation and the minority reading that permits wiping only part of the head, and explains مالك's reasoning for the majority المالكي position. On the المالكي requirement of muwalah (continuity) in الوضوء — performing the obligatory elements one بعد another without المهم interruption — Ibn Abd al-Barr presents the evidence from the practice of النبي ﷺ and the الصحابة, which مالك regarded as establishing continuity as an الالتزام rather than merely a السنة. The practical question of how long an interruption is 'المهم' is addressed through the principle of custom: an interruption that causes the earlier washed parts to dry قبل the later ones are washed is أيضاً long. For الغسل, Ibn Abd al-Barr presents the الحديث on the obligating causes and the minimum valid performance. His characteristic approach is to note the sound hadiths that establish each ruling and to explain how مالك interpreted them. On the question of whether the الغسل of janabah and the Friday الغسل may be combined in one bath, he presents مالك's position (they may be combined with a single intention for both) with the evidential basis. The الباب on hayd (menstruation) is presented with the clarity that reflects Ibn Abd al-Barr's awareness of its practical importance. The المالكي positions on the minimum and maximum durations of hayd, the characteristics that distinguish menstrual blood from other types of bleeding, and the acts forbidden to a menstruating woman are all stated with their evidential grounding.
